text: It is likely enough that the trees are represented as bearing twelve different kinds of fruit, but St John’s language does not make this certain.
‘The angel said to me’; literally, ‘he said to me’.
‘The command came to me’; literally, ‘he said to me’. The context seems to indicate that our Lord is here the speaker, not the Angel, as in verse 6.

Some Greek manuscripts have ‘those who carry out his commandments’, instead of ‘those who wash their garments’. The words ‘in the blood of the Lamb’ are not given in the Greek, or in most Latin manuscripts.
For ‘the book of life’, the Greek (followed by many Latin manuscripts) has ‘the tree of life’; our text probably arises from a mistake in the Latin.
The words ‘Be it so’ represent ‘Amen’ in the Greek, which is attached by some commentators to the sentence which goes before it.
The best reading in the Greek here is ‘with the saints’, or ‘with all the saints’, not ‘with you all’; many of the Greek manuscripts omit the word ‘Amen’.
